Ada of Scotland (died after 1206), also known as Ada of Huntingdon, was a member of the Scottish royal house who became Countess of Holland by marriage to Floris III, Count of Holland.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland於1140年出生在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾吉波爾頓時，她的父親Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria26歲，母親Ada de Warenne20歲。她於1162年8月28日與Floris III van Holland結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1204年之後在荷蘭藍德米德爾包格，埋葬在荷蘭藍德米德爾包格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland于1140年出生在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾吉波爾頓时，她的父亲Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria26岁，母亲Ada de Warenne20岁。她于1162年8月28日与Floris III van Holland结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和8个女儿的父母。她去世于1204年之后在荷兰藍德米德爾包格，埋葬在荷兰藍德米德爾包格。

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Ada
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin; it was not in general use before the late 18th century. It may be a Latinate variant of the biblical name Adah . However, it has also been explained as a pet form of Adele and Adelaide , Germanic female names of which the first element is adal ‘noble’. It was borne by a 7th-century abbess of Saint-Julien-des-Prés at Le Mans.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Huntingdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Huntington  .

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

en.lifeSummary: When Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland was born in 1140, in Kimbolton, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, was 26 and her mother, Ada de Warenne, was 20. She married Floris III van Holland on 28 August 1162. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 8 daughters. She died after 1204, in Middelburg, Zeeland, Netherlands, and was buried in Middelburg, Zeeland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland est née en 1140, à Kimbolton, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Ada de Warenne, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Floris III van Holland le 28 août 1162. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 8 filles. Elle est décédée après 1204, à Middelburg, Zélande, Pays-Bas, et a été enterrée à Middelburg, Zélande, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland 1140 in Kimbolton, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria 26 und ihre Mutter Ada de Warenne 20. Sie hat am 28. August 1162 Floris III van Holland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1204 in Middelburg, Seeland, Niederlande und wurde in Middelburg, Seeland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland è nata il nel 1140 nel Kimbolton, Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Ada de Warenne, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Floris III van Holland il 28 agosto 1162. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 8 figlie. Morì dopo il 1204, nel Middelburg, Zelanda, Paesi Bassi, e fu sepolta a Middelburg, Zelanda, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Hollandが1140年にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアキンボルトンで生まれた時，父Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbriaは26歳，母Ada de Warenneは20歳であった。1162年8月28日にFloris III van Hollandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼女は1204年以後にオランダゼエランドミッドエルブルグで死去し，オランダゼエランドミッドエルブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland(이)가 1140년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 킴볼턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ada de Warenne(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1162년 8월 28일 Floris III van Holland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1204년 이후 네덜란드 제랜드 미델버그에서 사망했으며 네덜란드 제랜드 미델버그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland nasceu em 1140, em Kimbolton, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Ada de Warenne, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Floris III van Holland em 28 de agosto de 1162. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 8 filhas. Ela faleceu após 1204, em Middelburg, Zelandia, Países Baixos, e foi sepultada em Middelburg, Zelandia, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland родилась в 1140 г. в местности кимболтон, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Ada de Warenne, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Floris III van Holland 28 августа 1162 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Она умерла после 1204 г. в местности мидделбург, зиланд, Нидерланды и была похоронена в местности мидделбург, зиланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland nació en 1140, en Kimbolton, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Ada de Warenne, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Floris III van Holland el 28 de agosto de 1162. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió después de 1204, en Middelburg, Zeeland, Países Bajos, y fue sepultada en Middelburg, Zeeland, Países Bajos.
